notes: Nuance on VOID semantics: VOID does NOT mean 'candidate failed to beat baseline' (that is REJECT). VOID means the PROBE itself is inadmissible/unobservable — i.e., the gate cannot kill its own identity baseline (probe.py:77-81, ProbeInadmissible docstring 'cannot falsify'), OR a metric is missing. The landing's parenthetical 'VOID = failed to beat baseline' (index.html:577) is INACCURATE: failing to beat baseline is REJECT; VOID = the gate-that-cannot-kill-the-baseline-is-inadmissible case, which IS real and correctly implemented.

Mem0 default UPDATE_MEMORY_PROMPT deletes contradicting facts (https://github.com/mem0ai/mem0/issues/4536). It is, however, the central design principle of Zep/Graphiti, a popular framework-agnostic memory layer. MUTATORS confirmed: Letta agents self-edit/overwrite memory blocks (full replacement, last-write-wins) https://www.letta.com/blog/memory-blocks ; CrewAI consolidation does keep/update/DELETE + forget() https://docs.crewai.com/en/concepts/memory + https://blog.crewai.com/how-we-built-cognitive-memory-for-agentic-systems/ ; Mem0 default OSS UPDATE_MEMORY_PROMPT DELETEs contradicting facts (and per issue #4536 does not re-add) https://github.com/mem0ai/mem0/blob/main/mem0/configs/prompts.py + https://github.com/mem0ai/mem0/issues/4536 . IMMUTABLE-SOURCE confirmed: Graphiti/Zep — episodes = raw ground-truth, facts invalidated-not-deleted, bi-temporal, full lineage https://github.com/getzep/graphiti + https://arxiv.org/html/2501.13956v1 . Adversarial findings refine but do NOT overturn: (1) Mem0 is partly mischaracterized — its graph variant Mem0ᵍ and Platform v3 temporal reasoning mark conflicting relationships INVALID not deleted, preserving history (https://arxiv.org/html/2504.19413v1, https://docs.mem0.ai/platform/features/temporal-reasoning), so Mem0 straddles both columns. (3) Even Graphiti has a destructive remove_episode()/delete_episode() that cascade-deletes edges + orphaned nodes (https://github.com/getzep/graphiti/blob/main/mcp_server/README.md, issue #1083) — so its immutability is fact-level, not absolute. notes: The headline 'most agent stacks haven't picked up a single SQLite file' is weakest part: four of the ten in-scope frameworks ship SQLite-backed, sqlite3-openable, file-based persistence today. PARTIAL because the *scope* of what BENE unifies in one file is still rare (next findings).

refute.basis: Original PARTIAL verdict holds and is reinforced. BROAD claim ("most agent stacks haven't picked up SQLite as agent state") is OVERSTATED — all 4 cited frameworks verified current: LangGraph SqliteSaver/AsyncSqliteSaver (reference.langchain.com/python/langgraph.checkpoint.sqlite/SqliteSaver; pypi.org/project/langgraph-checkpoint-sqlite), OpenAI Agents SDK SQLiteSession/AdvancedSQLiteSession "for persistent storage provide a file path" (openai.github.io/openai-agents-python/sessions/), CrewAI long-term memory in SQLite "inspect the SQLite databases directly" (deepwiki.com/crewAIInc/crewAI/7.2-memory-configuration-and-storage), Google ADK DatabaseSessionService sqlite+aiosqlite (github.com/google/adk-python/blob/v1.23.0/src/google/adk/sessions/sqlite_session_service.py). NEW stronger refutation found: Turso AgentFS (github.com/tursodatabase/agentfs) markets BENE's EXACT narrow framing — "entire agent runtime—files, state, history—stored in a single SQLite file," "Snapshot...with cp agent.db snapshot.db," "Query your agent's complete history with SQL," runs serverless — proving the "single portable file/cp/no-server/SQL-audit" idea is a named 2026 pattern, not a gap (also joeltd.com "SQLite Is Becoming The Agent State Default"; Nathan Hamlett DEV "entire state layer is a single SQLite file" in production). BUT BENE's genuinely narrow differentiator survives: none of these bundle state+cross-agent-decisions+traces+eval-verdicts+trust in ONE server-free file — OpenAI SQLiteSession is "one option among several...conversational exchanges, not eval verdicts/trust/cross-agent decisions"; LangGraph pairs with LangSmith server/dashboard for traces+evals (the "dashboard you're waiting on" BENE contrasts). AgentFS is BETA and filesystem-scoped (no eval/trust union). So not FALSE; the broad framing overstates but the narrow multi-domain union is real and uncommon. PARTIAL is correct.

claim: BENE is novel in writing ALL of {agent state + cross-agent decisions (SharedLog) + searchable traces (engrams) + eval verdicts (kill gates) + trust signals} onto ONE SQLite file (BENE landing: 'BENE writes the receipts, the kill gate, and the trust signal into code and onto a single SQLite file ... No portal') and :738 ('one SQLite file · sqlite3 / cp / rsync / diff just work'). OpenAI SQLiteSession is 'a message log — it doesn't extract facts' (https://hindsight.vectorize.io/blog/2026/04/17/openai-agents-persistent-memory). So no in-scope mainstream framework unifies state+decisions+traces+evals+trust in one file — BUT purpose-built (non-mainstream) systems DO ship exactly this pitch: Turso AgentFS 'manage all their state in a single SQLite database ... portable ... fully auditable ... every file operation, tool call, and state change is recorded in SQLite' (https://turso.tech/blog/agentfs); memweave 'entire store is a single file on disk — no server, no Docker, no cloud service' with git-diffable provenance (https://towardsdatascience.com/memweave-zero-infra-ai-agent-memory-with-markdown-and-sqlite-no-vector-database-required).

It is NOT unique in the broader ecosystem (Turso AgentFS / memweave do the same single-file pitch), but those are niche, not 'popular agentic frameworks', so they don't refute BENE's specific 'most agent stacks' wording. refute.basis: Re-check confirms PARTIAL on both halves. Mainstream-split half CONFIRMED: LangSmith docs state "Regardless of the checkpointer backend, LangSmith always requires PostgreSQL for threads, runs, assistants, crons, and the memory store" with traces/evals in GCP-hosted cloud (https://docs.langchain.com/langsmith/configure-checkpointer); CrewAI keeps genuinely separate stores in separate dirs — ChromaDB (short-term+entity RAG) vs SQLite3 (long-term task outcomes), no trace/eval/trust unification (https://deepwiki.com/crewAIInc/crewAI/7.2-memory-configuration-and-storage). Purpose-built counter-candidates that keep this from being clean novelty CONFIRMED and EXPANDED: Turso AgentFS = one SQLite file with fs+KV-state+tool-call audit trail but NO eval verdicts/kill gates/trust/cross-agent decisions (~2 of 5) (https://turso.tech/blog/agentfs); NEW candidate agent-replay (clay-good/agent-replay) is "100% local, single SQLite file" storing traces+eval verdicts+guardrail safety policies in the same DB (~3 of 5) (https://github.com/clay-good/agent-replay). BENE claim verified verbatim at site/index.html:688 and :738. Crucially, NO system found (mainstream or purpose-built) unifies ALL FIVE of BENE's specific concerns (state + cross-agent SharedLog decisions + engram traces + sha256-hash-locked kill-gate verdicts + computed trust ledger) in one file — agent-replay gets closest at 3/5 but is a trace-ingesting debug CLI, not a build-on framework with native multi-agent state. So BENE's narrow on-page claim ('writes the receipts, the kill gate, and the trust signal onto a single SQLite file') is defensible vs mainstream, but the implied 'one auditable SQLite file for agents' is a crowded, documented 2026 design pattern. Original evidence was well-cited and URLs verified. Verdict not refuted.

claim: Popular frameworks' state persistence is server-backed / requires a client library or portal, unlike BENE's no-client, no-portal file
evidence: Strongest support from Letta/MemGPT (server-first): 'Letta treats agents as persistent services, maintaining state server-side ... behind a REST API' and production uses Postgres/Aurora ('All agent state lives in a database (Postgres or SQLite)') — https://blog.stackademic.com/letta-platform-for-stateful-llm-agents-a83b58a1c926 and https://aws.amazon.com/blogs/database/how-letta-builds-production-ready-ai-agents-with-amazon-aurora-postgresql . Pydantic AI durability is server/Postgres-backed: DBOS 'uses Postgres as its only infrastructure dependency' (https://pydantic.dev/articles/pydantic-ai-dbos); Restate persists message history in 'Restate's durable K/V store' (https://pydantic.dev/articles/restate-durable-execution-pydanticai). LangGraph production Agent Server: 'PostgreSQL is always required' + traces to LangSmith SaaS (https://docs.langchain.com/langsmith/data-plane). COUNTER-EVIDENCE that no-client/no-portal file is NOT unique: AutoGen save_state/load_state writes a plain JSON file you 'json.dump ... to disk' with no client (https://microsoft.github.io/autogen/stable//user-guide/agentchat-user-guide/tutorial/state.html); LlamaIndex Context.to_dict() is 'a JSON-serializable dict ... carry state across runs' (https://developers.llamaindex.ai/python/workflows-api-reference/context); ADK SQLite file is openable with sqlite3 though it 'requires an async database driver' (https://adk.dev/sessions/session). So 'requires a portal/client' is true for Letta/Pydantic-AI-durable/LangGraph-server but false for AutoGen/ADK/OpenAI-SDK/LlamaIndex local files.
notes: Spectrum: Letta = server+portal (BENE claim holds); Pydantic AI durable + LangGraph-in-production = Postgres + cloud traces (claim holds for production path); AutoGen JSON / ADK SQLite / OpenAI SQLiteSession / LlamaIndex dict = local, no-portal, no-client (claim OVERSTATED). Mixed => PARTIAL.

refute.basis: Claim says popular frameworks' state persistence is server-backed / needs a client library or portal, vs BENE's no-client no-portal file. Verified 2026 docs show the dominant majority of popular frameworks ship NATIVE no-server, no-client, no-portal file persistence — the exact feature BENE claims as differentiator: OpenAI Agents SDK SQLiteSession(session_id, db_path) writes a plain local .db, 'requires no external infrastructure' / 'survives process restarts without any external infrastructure' (https://openai.github.io/openai-agents-python/sessions/); AutoGen save_state/load_state -> json.dump to disk, no server (https://microsoft.github.io/autogen/stable//user-guide/agentchat-user-guide/tutorial/state.html); LlamaIndex Context.to_dict() JSON dict 'requiring neither a persistent server nor special client architecture' (https://developers.llamaindex.ai/python/workflows-api-reference/context); Google ADK DatabaseSessionService sqlite+aiosqlite:///./file.db local file + InMemorySessionService (https://adk.dev/sessions/session). Most damning: LangGraph — cited by the original verdict on the SERVER-BACKED side ('PostgreSQL is always required') — actually ships first-party SqliteSaver to a local .db that 'requires no separate server process' (https://docs.langchain.com/oss/python/langgraph/persistence). Only Letta is genuinely server-first, and even it exports a portable .af Agent File and supports SQLite (https://github.com/letta-ai/letta). So the generalization is false for OpenAI-SDK/AutoGen/LlamaIndex/LangGraph/ADK; BENE's single-file SQLite persistence is a commodity feature, not a differentiator. Original PARTIAL is too generous: the claim's core generalization inverts reality -> OVERSTATED.

claim: Cross-agent decisions are durably recorded in the same auditable file (BENE SharedLog: intent -> vote -> decide), which popular frameworks lack
evidence: BENE claim at /home/admin/gh/bene-main/site/index.html:363 ('cross-agent decisions ride SharedLog (intent -> vote -> decide)' on the SQLite VFS). Mainstream multi-agent frameworks persist conversation/team state but do NOT record a structured cross-agent decision ledger as first-class auditable rows: AutoGen team save_state 'saves the state of all the agents in the team' as one JSON blob, no per-decision intent/vote/decide schema (https://microsoft.github.io/autogen/stable//user-guide/agentchat-user-guide/tutorial/state.html); CrewAI persists task outputs/memory but routes decision observability to its platform traces (https://docs.crewai.com/en/changelog). LangGraph captures 'agent routing decisions' but in LangSmith traces (cloud), not as a portable decision ledger in the checkpoint file (https://www.langchain.com/blog/announcing-the-langchain-mongodb-partnership...). Letta returns 'all steps including intermediate reasoning and tool calls' for audit but server-side via API (https://blog.stackademic.com/letta-platform-for-stateful-llm-agents-a83b58a1c926). So a structured, in-file, vote-based cross-agent decision record is genuinely uncommon — but the broader 'event-sourcing / append-only decision log in a local file' pattern is independently advocated and shipped (LlamaIndex discussion: 'Event sourcing pattern - log all state transitions'; HF forum: 'Every agent step writes an event into a durable session log' — https://github.com/run-llama/llama_index/discussions/20381 , https://discuss.huggingface.co/t/how-do-you-preserve-agent-state-across-restarts/172174).
notes: BENE's intent->vote->decide SharedLog as a first-class auditable table is the most differentiated sub-claim of G4 vs the named frameworks; downgraded to PARTIAL (not VERIFIED) because (a) the general append-only decision/event-log-in-a-file pattern is well known and recommended in these ecosystems, and (b) BENE's own /home/admin/gh/bene-main/site/index.html:688 concedes the realistic stance: 'Whatever's on top — LangGraph, CrewAI, your own loop — is fine,' i.e. BENE positions as a drop-in receipts layer, implicitly acknowledging the host frameworks exist and persist state.

refute.basis: BENE claim verified at /home/admin/gh/bene-main/site/index.html:363 ('cross-agent decisions ride SharedLog (intent → vote → decide)'). Adversarial 2026 search found NO popular framework that natively ships a structured, in-file, vote-based cross-agent decision ledger, so the novelty survives: (1) AutoGen save_state = single JSON state blob, voting is app-level only, no per-decision intent/vote/decide schema (microsoft.github.io/autogen state.html); (2) CrewAI 'consensual'/'consensus voting' process is an UNIMPLEMENTED enum that raises NotImplementedError — PR #1926 'planned, not implemented yet' (github.com/crewAIInc/crewAI/pull/1926) — i.e. vaporware, no vote ledger; (3) LangGraph checkpoint IS a portable durable in-file record (SQLite/Postgres) of Command(goto=...) routing, but it is a per-superstep STATE SNAPSHOT, not a structured per-decision ledger, and LangGraph has ZERO cross-agent voting primitive (docs.langchain.com persistence; reference.langchain.com checkpoints); (4) DeALOG (arXiv 2602.00996) is an academic paper with a natural-language append-only shared log, not structured intent/vote/decide, not a popular framework; (5) broad 2026 sweep surfaces only arXiv proposals (Policy Decision Record + Transaction Intent Schema, arXiv 2601.04583; Right-to-History sovereignty kernel, arXiv 2602.20214) and vendor governance blogs — none a shipping popular framework bundling native first-class append-only intent→vote→decide in one DB file. PARTIAL (not VERIFIED) is correct because: BENE's SPECIFIC structured vote-ledger form is genuinely uncommon, but the weaker generic 'append-only event/decision log in a local file' pattern is independently advocated/shipped (LangGraph local-SQLite checkpoint; LlamaIndex/HF event-sourcing discussions). One imprecision in the original evidence (it said LangGraph keeps routing decisions only in cloud LangSmith traces — in fact the checkpoint is a portable LOCAL-file record) is exactly the kind of overstatement that keeps the claim at PARTIAL rather than fully VERIFIED, but does not refute it since the checkpoint still is not a cross-agent vote ledger.
